title: Regeneron and Intellia Therapeutics Expand Collaboration to Develop CRISPR/Cas9-Based Treatments
text: Intellia Therapeutics and Regeneron Pharmaceuticals announced an expansion of their existing collaboration to provide Regeneron with rights to develop products for additional in vivo CRISPR/Cas9-based therapeutic targets and for the companies to jointly develop potential products for the treatment of hemophilia A and B. Regeneron also received non-exclusive rights to independently develop and commercialize ex vivo gene edited products. In the initial 2016 partnership, Regeneron paid $75 million upfront, as well as milestone payments and royalties. In a 2020 expansion, Intellia received an upfront payment of $70 million, and Regeneron made an additional equity investment in Intellia of $30 million at $32.42 per share. Per the terms of the original agreement, Regeneron will pay potential royalties and milestone payments for the in vivo products it independently develops. Under the previous 2016 agreement, the collaboration could yield up to $320 million in milestones and royalties for Intellia.
